Chunk # 0.
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113 Gastroenterology and Hepatology From Bed to Bench   
©2016 RIGLD , Research Institute for Gastro enterology and Liver Diseases   
 
 
Comparison of three methods for mi tochondria isolation from the 
human liver cell line ( HepG2 )  
Pedram Azimzadeh1, Hamid Asadzadeh Aghdaei2, Peyman Tarban2, Mohammad Mahdi Akhondi1, Abolfazl 
Shirazi1, Hamid Reza Khorram Khorshid3  
1Reproductive Biotechnology Research Center, Avice nna Research Institute, ACECR, Tehran, Iran  
2 Basic and Molecular Epidemiology of Gastrointestinal Diso rders, Research Institute for Gastroenterology and Liver 
Diseases, Shahid Beheshti University of Medical Sciences, Tehran, Iran 
3Genetic Research Center, Univer sity of Social Welfare and Rehabilitation Sciences, Tehran, Iran  
 
ABSTRACT 
Aim: The aim of this study was to evaluate and compare th ree available methods for mitochondrial isolation from a 
human cell line to predict the best me thod for each probable application 
 Background : Organelle isolation is gaining importance in expe rimental laboratory settings Mitochondrial dysfunction 
may affect tumorgenesis process There are some evidences th at transplantation of healthy, intact and active mitochondria 
into cells containing defective mitochondria may reduce the pr oliferation Therefore, isolated mitochondria could be 
considered as an effective tool for assessment a nd management of mitochondrial related disorders  
Patients and methods : Mitochondrial isolation from the human liver cell line ( HepG2 ) was performed using two 
commercially available kits, including Qproteome (Qiagen)  and MITOISO2 (Sigma-Aldrich), as well as a manual 
method Integrity of inner membrane of mitochondria was assessed by JC-1 staining Activity of isolated mitochondria was evaluated by DCFH-DA staining, and total yield of isolated mitochondria determined by micro-Lowry method Finally, relative quantification using Real-time PCR wa s conducted to compare th e mtDNA copy number of 
mitochondria isolated by three different methods  
Results : Compared to other methods, manual kit resulted in higher yields of total amount of mitochondrial protein and 
mtDNA copy numbers Isolated mitochondria by Qproteome kit, showed a higher activity Finally, the integrity of inner-membrane of isolated mitochondria was significantly higher in Qproteome when compared with the other two methods  Conclusion : Due to differences in quality, quantity and activity of isolated mitochondria using three techniques 
discussed here, the method in which best-suited to each resear ch project should be select ed according to the distinct 
features of isolated mitochondria   
Keywords : Mitochondrial isolation, Cell line, HepG2  
(Please cite as:  Azimzadeh P, Asadzadeh Aghdaei H, Tarban P, Akhondi MM, Shirazi A, Khorram Khorshid HR 
Comparison of Three methods for mitochondria  isolation from human liver cell line ( HepG2 ) Gastroenterol 
Hepatol Bed Bench  2016;9(2):105-113)  
 
Introduction   
  1Organelle analysis is a powerful strategy to 
discover the pathophysiology of human diseases 
                                                 
Received : 12 October 2015   Accepted : 18 December 2015 
Reprint or Correspondence : Hamid Reza Khorram 
Khorshid, MD Genetic Research Center, University of 
Social Welfare and Rehabilitation Sciences, Tehran, Iran 
E-mail: hrkk1@uswr.ac.ir  (1, 2) There are established methods for isolation 
of lysosomes, peroxisomes, endoplasmic reticulum and mitochondria (3-5) One of the most useful applications of the organelle isolation is to determine the localization of proteins within cells (6)  Proteomics analysis of mitochondria also ORIGINAL ARTICLE 106  Comparison of Three methods for mitochon dria isolation from human liver cell line 
 
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113  
 needs pure and intact isolated mitochondria in the 
first step (7) Mitochondrial proteome consists of 13 proteins encoded by mtDNA, which are involved in cellular respiration However, the rest of mitochondrial proteins are encoded by nuclear genome, synthesized in cytosolic ribosomes and imported into the organelle (8) It has been shown that the number of mitochondria varies among 
different cells (9, 10) Therefore, design and 
application of suitable mitochondria isolation methods for different types of cells with different amounts of mitochondria could be helpful (11, 12)  There are two main reasons for biomedical scientists to study about mitochondria, including: discovery of the genetic basis of several mitochondrial diseases and targeting mitochondria for gene therapy The next step in this process would be gene replacement to prevent from mitochondrial disorders (13) The second reason to study mitochondria is its main role in the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) (14) ROS production induces mitochondrial dysfunction, apoptosis and necrosis while cells undergoing aging course ROS production is also integrated with mitochondrial redox signaling (15) Accordingly, the amount of ROS produced by mitochondria is a marker for the determination of mitochondrial activity (15) ROS production can be detected using Dichloro-dihydro-fluorescein diacetate (DCFH-DA) dye DCFH-DA is a non-polar, non-fluorescent dye that turns to a highly fluorescent derivative of DCF when oxidized by H
2O2 or other ROS (16)  
The quantity of isolated mitochondria could be determined by assessment of total protein content and mtDNA copy number Two main methods are available for measurement of total protein content

mitochondria.protocol:
**Article Title:** Comparison of three methods for mitochondrial isolation from the human liver cell line (HepG2)

**Article Authors:** Pedram Azimzadeh, Hamid Asadzadeh Aghdaei, Peyman Tarban, Mohammad Mahdi Akhondi, Abolfazl Shirazi, Hamid Reza Khorram Khorshid

**Abstract:** The aim of this study was to evaluate and compare three available methods for mitochondrial isolation from a human cell line to predict the best method for each probable application. Organelle isolation is gaining importance in experimental laboratory settings, and mitochondrial dysfunction may affect tumorgenesis process. Isolated mitochondria could be considered as an effective tool for assessment and management of mitochondrial related disorders.

**Introduction:** Organelle analysis is a powerful strategy to discover the pathophysiology of human diseases. There are established methods for isolation of lysosomes, peroxisomes, endoplasmic reticulum, and mitochondria. One of the most useful applications of organelle isolation is to determine the localization of proteins within cells. Proteomics analysis of mitochondria also needs pure and intact isolated mitochondria in the first step. The quantity of isolated mitochondria could be determined by assessment of total protein content and mtDNA copy number.

**Methods:** Mitochondrial isolation from the human liver cell line (HepG2) was performed using two commercially available kits, including Qproteome (Qiagen) and MITOISO2 (Sigma-Aldrich), as well as a manual method. The integrity of the inner membrane of mitochondria was assessed by JC-1 staining. The activity of isolated mitochondria was evaluated by DCFH-DA staining. The total yield of isolated mitochondria was determined by the micro-Lowry method. Relative quantification using Real-time PCR was conducted to compare the mtDNA copy number of mitochondria isolated by the three different methods.

**Results:** Compared to other methods, the manual kit resulted in higher yields of the total amount of mitochondrial protein and mtDNA copy numbers. Isolated mitochondria by the Qproteome kit showed higher activity. Finally, the integrity of the inner membrane of isolated mitochondria was significantly higher in Qproteome when compared with the other two methods.

**Conclusion:** Due to differences in quality, quantity, and activity of isolated mitochondria using the three techniques discussed here, the method best-suited to each research project should be selected according to the distinct features of isolated mitochondria.

**Keywords:** Mitochondrial isolation, Cell line, HepG2

**Steps for Mitochondrial Isolation:**
1. Mitochondrial isolation from the human liver cell line (HepG2) was performed using two commercially available kits, including Qproteome (Qiagen) and MITOISO2 (Sigma-Aldrich), as well as a manual method.
2. The integrity of the inner membrane of mitochondria was assessed by JC-1 staining.
3. The activity of isolated mitochondria was evaluated by DCFH-DA staining.
4. The total yield of isolated mitochondria was determined by the micro-Lowry method.
5. Relative quantification using Real-time PCR was conducted to compare the mtDNA copy number of mitochondria isolated by the three different methods.

**Notes:**
- The article does not provide detailed step-by-step instructions for each method.
- The article does not mention the specific steps involved in the manual method.
- The article does not provide information on the duration or temperature of the isolation process.
- The article does not mention any centrifugation steps or specific buffers used in the isolation process.

**Conclusion:**
The article compares three methods for mitochondrial isolation from the human liver cell line (HepG2). The methods evaluated include two commercially available kits (Qproteome and MITOISO2) and a manual method. The article reports differences in quality, quantity

summary: Three methods for isolating mitochondria from a human liver cell line (HepG2) were compared. The manual kit resulted in higher yields of mitochondrial protein and mtDNA copy numbers. The Qproteome kit showed higher activity, while the integrity of the inner membrane was significantly higher in Qproteome compared to the other methods. The choice of method should be based on the specific features required for each research project.

article:
Chunk # 1.
Biuret and Lowry procedures, former is more prevalent in clinical tests and the latter is convenient for analytical works because of its higher sensitivity (17)  Preservation of membrane integrity is another 
important factor during mitochondria isolation 
TMRM, Rhod123, JC-1 and DiOC
6 are typical 
probes for measurement of mitochondrial membrane potential JC-1 is a widely used dye for measurement of inner-membrane potential of isolated mitochondria (18) Some evidences show the transplantation of 
healthy, intact and active mitochondria into cells 
containing defective ones could be used as a potential healing agent for  a variety of diseases (19) Therefore, we need a reliable source of mitochondria for isolation and available source of 
recipient cells For further genetics or biochemical analysis, human cell lines are considered as a good source for mitochondria isolation, due to their easy identification, cultivation and availability (20)  The aim of this study was to compare three available methods for mitochondrial isolation with regard to their suitability for different requirements on isolated mitochondria  
 
Patients and Methods 
Cell Culture 
Human liver cell line HepG2  was obtained 
from National Cell Bank ( Pasteur  Institute of 
Iran) HepG2 cells were  cultured in 25 cm2 tissue 
culture flasks (SPL, China) containing RPMI 1640 media (Gibco, USA), supplemented with 10% heat 
inactivated Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS) (Gibco, 
USA), and 1% (v/v) penicillin-streptomycin (Sigma-Aldrich, USA) Adherent cells were detached using trypsin-EDTA solution (Gibco, USA), containing 0.05 % trypsin and 0.5 mM EDTA, at 80% confluence Cells were counted and centrifuged at 2000 rpm (R: 10.70)  
Isolation of Mitochondria  
Mitochondrial isolation was performed using 
three available methods, including two commercial kits and one manual method Qproteum mitochondria is olation kit (Qiagen, 
USA), and MITOISO2 mitochondria isolation kit  Azimzadeh P et al  107 
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113  (Sigma, USA) were commercial kits The manual 
isolation method was perfo rmed using differential 
centrifugation as previously described by Dhruv Sareen, et al  (21) with some modifications 
Isolation basis for two selected kits are quite related, but including them in such analyzes may help to elucidate the best way to use them  
Briefly, we used the same extraction buffer, but 
in the homogenization step dounce homogenizer 
was used to have a gentler treatment with mitochondria exerted from cells Another 
difference was in the buffer in which activity assay was performed We have found a respiratory buffer described by Mashayekhi et al  (22) more 
practical with more clear results MOPS, MgCl
2, 
Sodium succinate and EGTA were commonly found in two buffers However, the respiratory buffer includes KH
2PO 4 instead of KCl   
Quantity of Isolated Mitochondria  
Total protein content has been considered as a 
marker for quantity of isolated mitochondria (4) To assess the micrograms of isolated mitochondrial protein, there were two major choices, including: Biuret and Lowry methods Biuret is more suitable for high concentrations of mitochondria and the Lowry method instead recommended due to its high sensitivity (23) The micro-Lowry method with Onishi and Barr Modification (TP0200, Sigma-Aldrich, USA) was used according to manufacturer’s instructions Briefly, this method is based on two main reactions, including: biuret reaction and a reduction reaction that yields a purple color Finally, reading the absorbance of the colored solution at 500 nm and 800 nm provided a raw data Then, a standard curve delineated and 
micrograms of unknown samples were determined To draw the standard curve, a 400-µg/mL stock was diluted in water into 50, 100, 200 and 300 µg/mL aliquots After determining the protein concentration of each sample, results were multiplied by the dilution factor to obtain the total protein concentration of isolated 
mitochondria using each method  
Relative mtDNA copy numbers of isolated 
mitochondria by three methods were also measured using a SYBR green based quantitative real-time PCR assay as described before (24) Essential primers for detection and amplification of mtDNA were designed for tRNA
Leu (UUR) gene 
(Table-1) All tests were repeated three times, conducted in triplicate and β-2-microglobulin was 
used as an endogenous control for normalization of relative quantification test results (24) The ROX fluorescent dye was added to the real-time PCR master mix as a background dye to normalize the fluorescent signals in each reaction (25)  
DNA was extracted QIAamp using DNA mini 
kit (Qiagen, USA) with some modifications Briefly, mtDNA was extracted according to manufacturer’s instructions; however, RNase A treatment was eliminated This is due to the negative effect of RNase treatment on mtDNA that is mainly a DNA-RNA hybrid RNase treatment probably is the way that ribonucleotides are in contact with close circular mtDNA and consequently lower the rate of mtDNA yield (26-29) All PCR procedures were performed using RNase Free material and plastic-ware 
Quantitative PCR mix (Power SYBR® Green 
 
Table 1  Sequence of primers used for quantitative Real time PCR

mitochondria.protocol:
**Mitochondrial Isolation Steps:**

1. Mitochondrial isolation was performed using three available methods, including two commercial kits and one manual method.
2. The commercial kits used were the Qproteum mitochondria isolation kit (Qiagen, USA) and the MITOISO2 mitochondria isolation kit (Sigma, USA).
3. The manual isolation method was performed using differential centrifugation as previously described by Dhruv Sareen, et al. with some modifications.
4. In the homogenization step, a dounce homogenizer was used to have a gentler treatment with mitochondria exerted from cells.
5. The extraction buffer used was the same for all methods.
6. The activity assay was performed using a respiratory buffer described by Mashayekhi et al., which includes MOPS, MgCl2, Sodium succinate, and EGTA.
7. The respiratory buffer includes KH2PO4 instead of KCl.
8. The quantity of isolated mitochondria was assessed using total protein content as a marker.
9. The Biuret and Lowry methods were used to assess the micrograms of isolated mitochondrial protein.
10. The micro-Lowry method with Onishi and Barr Modification (TP0200, Sigma-Aldrich, USA) was used according to the manufacturer's instructions.
11. The absorbance of the colored solution at 500 nm and 800 nm was read to obtain raw data.
12. A standard curve was drawn using a 400-µg/mL stock diluted in water into 50, 100, 200, and 300 µg/mL aliquots.
13. The protein concentration of each sample was determined, and the results were multiplied by the dilution factor to obtain the total protein concentration of isolated mitochondria using each method.
14. Relative mtDNA copy numbers of isolated mitochondria were measured using a SYBR green based quantitative real-time PCR assay.
15. Primers for detection and amplification of mtDNA were designed for the tRNA Leu (UUR) gene.
16. All tests were repeated three times, conducted in triplicate.
17. β-2-microglobulin was used as an endogenous control for normalization of relative quantification test results.
18. The ROX fluorescent dye was added to the real-time PCR master mix as a background dye to normalize the fluorescent signals in each reaction.
19. DNA was extracted using the QIAamp DNA mini kit (Qiagen, USA) with some modifications.
20. RNase A treatment was eliminated to avoid the negative effect on mtDNA yield.
21. All PCR procedures were performed using RNase Free material and plastic-ware.

**Incompleteness:**
- The article does not provide specific details about the steps involved in the differential centrifugation method.
- The article does not provide specific details about the modifications made to the manual isolation method.
- The article does not provide specific details about the SYBR green based quantitative real-time PCR assay.

**Conclusion:**
The article provides a detailed description of the mitochondrial isolation steps using three different methods: two commercial kits and one manual method. However, there are some protocol gaps and missing details, particularly regarding the differential centrifugation method, modifications to the manual isolation method, and the SYBR green based quantitative real-time PCR assay. Additional information would be needed to fully replicate the procedures.

summary:
Key Concepts:
1. Biuret and Lowry procedures for mitochondrial isolation
2. Membrane integrity during mitochondria isolation
3. Probes for measuring mitochondrial membrane potential
4. Transplantation of healthy mitochondria as a potential healing agent
5. Human cell lines as a good source for mitochondria isolation
6. Three methods compared for mitochondrial isolation
7. Cell culture and isolation of mitochondria
8. Quantity of isolated mitochondria measured using Biuret and Lowry methods
9. Measurement of relative mtDNA copy numbers using real-time PCR assay
10. DNA extraction for mtDNA analysis

SPR:
1. Biuret and Lowry procedures are used for mitochondrial isolation, with Biuret being more prevalent in clinical tests and Lowry being more convenient for analytical works due to its higher sensitivity.
2. Preservation of membrane integrity is crucial during mitochondria isolation.
3. TMRM, Rhod123, JC-1, and DiOC6 are typical probes for measuring mitochondrial membrane potential, with JC-1 being widely used for measuring inner-membrane potential of isolated mitochondria.
4. Transplantation of healthy mitochondria into cells with defective ones shows potential as a healing agent for various diseases.
5. Human cell lines are considered a reliable source for mitochondria isolation due to their easy identification, cultivation, and availability.
6. Three methods for mitochondrial isolation were compared for their suitability for different requirements on isolated mitochondria.
7. Mitochondrial isolation was performed using commercial kits and a manual method involving differential centrifugation.
8. The quantity of isolated mitochondria was measured using the Biuret and Lowry methods, with the Lowry method recommended for its high sensitivity.
9. Relative mtDNA copy numbers of isolated mitochondria were measured using a real-time PCR assay with SYBR green.
10. DNA extraction for mtDNA analysis was performed using a DNA mini kit with modifications to eliminate RNase treatment.

Note: The SPR has been condensed and simplified for brevity while preserving the core concepts.

 108  Comparison of Three methods for mitochon dria isolation from human liver cell line 
 
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113  
 PCR Master Mix) was obtained from ABI (Life 
technologies, USA) and primers were synthesized by Macrogen Co (Korea) with additional High-P erformance Liquid 
Chromatography (HPLC) purification  
Relative quantification was performed using 
ABI7500 Real-time PCR system (Applied Biosystems, USA) To confirm the quality and 
efficiency of PCR amplification, a dissociation 
step (Melt curve analysis) after regular amplification cycles with six serial dilutions of extracted mtDNA was performed High quality and efficient amplification should demonstrate at least one peak in melt curve analysis and the slope of standard curve w as used to calculate the 
efficiency as previously described (30) 
The relative mtDNA copy number was 
calculated using the Delta C
T method (24) The 
CT value of each sample, which obtained from 
amplification of tRNALeu(UUR) gene was 
normalized with a C T value of β-2-microglobulin 
to create the Delta C T To calculate the Delta C T 
value and relative mtDNA content, each method was considered as calibrator once and compared the amounts of mtDNA yielded from two other methods with the calibrator  
Quality and Membrane Integrity of Isolated 
Mitochondria 
Quality of isolated mitochondria and the 
integrity of membrane were assessed using the JC-1 test by Mitochondria Staining Kit (CS0390, Sigma, USA) This test is based on electrochemical proton gr adient of mitochondrial 
inner membrane (18, 31)  
Activity of Isolated Mitochondria  
ROS production was measured using 2’, 7’-
Dichloro-dihydro-fluor escein diacetate (DCFH-
DA) (Sigma, USA) assay This could be considered as a marker for mitochondri al activity 
due to production of hydrogen peroxide (H
2O2) 
and some other ROS DCFH-DA produces green fluorescence in the presence of ROS
 (32) As mentioned before, the respiratory buffer was used 
as a reaction medium to assay the amount of ROS released from mitochondria  
Briefly, we added DCFH-DA to the isolated 
mitochondria (0.4 µg of protein) in a final concentration of 10 µM and incubated for 15 minutes at room temperature Emission of fluorescent was determined using the BD FACS 
calibure (Becton Dickinson, Anaheim, CA), and 
flow cytometer Fluorescent intensity was determined for 10000 counts     
Statistical Analysis 
Comparison of quantitative variables such as 
total protein content, fluorescent emission of 
DCFH-DA dye and JC-1 dye between three groups of samples (Isolated using three different methods) were conducted using one-way ANOVA (Kruskal-Wallis) test Data were analyzed and plotted using ABI7500 software ver.2.0.1 (Life technologies, USA) and GraphPad Prism ver 5.0 (GraphPad Software Inc., La Jolla, CA) 
 
 
Results 
Cultured HepG2  cells were confluent in day 1, 
3 and 6 (Figure 1) Mitochondrial isolation was conducted by three methods on triplicate reactions  Measurement of  total protein content 
of isolated mitochondria (from 10
6 cultured cells) 
using micro-Lowry method revealed that manual 
differential centrifugation method provides more mitochondria (58.03 µg) th an the MITOISO2 kit 
(43.9 µg) Among these methods, Qproteum kit (37.6 µg) had the lowest yield (Figure 2) The difference between the total protein content of Qproteome kit and manual method was statistically significant (P= 0.032) However, no significant difference was observed between MITOISO2 and Qproteome kits  Azimzadeh P et al  109 
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113  
 
Figure 2 Comparison of total protein yield among 
isolated mitochondria with three methods 
 
Relative mtDNA copy number calculation 
revealed that mtDNA content of manual method was 3.4 fold and 2.2 fold higher than Qproteome and MITOISO2 kits, respec tively (Figure 3) These 
findings confirmed result s of protein assay  
Comparison of mitochondrial membrane 
integrity was performed using JC-1 staining and showed Qproteome kit provides more intact mitochondria and the isolated mitochondria by manual method had the lowest rank of membrane integrity (Figure 4) The difference between Qproteome kit and manual method indicated as an absorbance ratio of 595/535 nm JC-1 was 
statistically significant (P= 0.032) However, MITOISO2 and Qproteome kits showed no significant difference (P>0.05)  Results of mitochondrial activity assays (Defined as fluorescent intensity per 10
6 cells) showed that 
mitochondria isolated using the manual method and the MITOISO2 kit have quite similar 
activities (P>0.05) Mitochondria isolated with 
Qproteome showed a statistically significant high fluorescent intensity when compared to other methods (P= 0.002) (Figure 5)  
 
Discussion 
Despite many available procedures for in-vitro  
analysis of mitochondria, investigating intact and active isolated mitochondria from different cell types offer more unmatched applications So far, many researchers have been used manual and kit-based organelle isolation methods In the present study, the attributes of isolated mitochondria by three common methods were  compared to answer 
this question We have found that manual mitochondrial isolation method yields more mtDNA copy numbers in comparison with two kit-based methods

mitochondria.protocol:
**Steps for Mitochondrial Isolation:**

1. "Measurement of total protein content of isolated mitochondria (from 10^6 cultured cells) using micro-Lowry method revealed that manual differential centrifugation method provides more mitochondria (58.03 µg) than the MITOISO2 kit (43.9 µg). Among these methods, Qproteum kit (37.6 µg) had the lowest yield." (Azimzadeh et al., 2016)

2. "Relative mtDNA copy number calculation revealed that mtDNA content of manual method was 3.4 fold and 2.2 fold higher than Qproteome and MITOISO2 kits, respectively." (Azimzadeh et al., 2016)

3. "Comparison of mitochondrial membrane integrity was performed using JC-1 staining and showed Qproteome kit provides more intact mitochondria and the isolated mitochondria by manual method had the lowest rank of membrane integrity." (Azimzadeh et al., 2016)

4. "Results of mitochondrial activity assays showed that mitochondria isolated using the manual method and the MITOISO2 kit have quite similar activities. Mitochondria isolated with Qproteome showed a statistically significant high fluorescent intensity when compared to other methods." (Azimzadeh et al., 2016)

**Incompleteness or Uncertainties:**

- The article does not provide a detailed step-by-step protocol for each method of mitochondrial isolation.
- The specific centrifugation speeds, times, and temperatures are not mentioned.
- The specific reagents and buffers used in each method are not described.
- The article does not mention the specific steps for cell lysis or homogenization.

**Conclusion:**

The article provides a comparison of three methods for mitochondrial isolation, but it does not provide a complete protocol for each method. The steps mentioned highlight the differences in total protein content, mtDNA copy number, membrane integrity, and mitochondrial activity between the methods. However, the protocol gaps make it difficult to replicate the experiments without additional information.

summary:
Revised Summary: SPR

- Primer Name Sequence Amplicon Size:
  - β-2-microglobulin Forward: TGCTGTCTCCATGTTTGATGTATCT
  - Reverse: TCTCTGCTCCCCACCTCTAAGT (86 bp)
  - tRNALeu (UUR) Forward: CACCCAAGAACAGGGTTTGT
  - Reverse: TGGCCATGGGTATGTTGTTA (107 bp)

- Comparison of Three methods for mitochondria isolation from human liver cell line:
  - PCR Master Mix obtained from ABI (Life technologies, USA)
  - Primers synthesized by Macrogen Co (Korea) with HPLC purification
  - Relative quantification performed using ABI7500 Real-time PCR system
  - Quality and efficiency of PCR amplification confirmed through melt curve analysis and standard curve slope calculation
  - Relative mtDNA copy number calculated using the Delta CT method
  - Quality and membrane integrity of isolated mitochondria assessed using the JC-1 test
  - Activity of isolated mitochondria measured through ROS production using DCFH-DA assay
  - Statistical analysis conducted using one-way ANOVA (Kruskal-Wallis) test

- Results:
  - Manual differential centrifugation method provides the highest yield of mitochondria (58.03 µg)
  - Qproteum kit has the lowest yield of mitochondria (37.6 µg)
  - Manual method has the highest mtDNA content, 3.4 fold higher than Qproteome kit and 2.2 fold higher than MITOISO2 kit
  - Qproteome kit provides the most intact mitochondria, while manual method has the lowest membrane integrity
  - Manual method and MITOISO2 kit show similar mitochondrial activity, while Qproteome kit exhibits higher activity

- Discussion:
  - Manual mitochondrial isolation method yields more mtDNA copy numbers compared to kit-based methods

Entities included in the revised summary:
1. High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC) is used for purification.
2. Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113 by Azimzadeh P et al.
3. The Mitochondria Staining Kit (CS0390) is used for staining.
4. Sigma is the supplier of the 2',hydrogen peroxide (H2O2).
5. BD FACS calibure by Becton Dickinson is a flow cytometer.
6. The ABI7500 software ver.2.0.1 is used for analysis.
7. GraphPad Prism ver 5.0 by GraphPad Software Inc. is used for statistical analysis.
8. Cultured HepG2 cells are used in the experiments.
9. The quality and membrane integrity of isolated mitochondria are assessed.
10. The activity of isolated mitochondria is measured.
11. Statistical analysis is performed on the data.
12. The forward primer for β-2-microglobulin is TGCTGTCTCCATGTTTGATGTATCT.
13. The reverse primer for β-2-microglobulin is TCTCTGCTCCCCACCTCTAAGT.
14. The forward primer for tRNALeu (UUR) is CACCCAAGAACAGGGTTTGT.
15. The reverse primer for tRNALeu (UUR) is TGGCCATGGGTATGTTGTTA.
16. Mitochondria isolation is performed.
17. Mitochondrial membrane integrity is assessed.
18. Mitochondrial activity is measured.
19. Protein content is quantified.
20. Fluorescent intensity is measured using JC-1 test and DCFH-DA.

Missing Entities: Anaheim conducted High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC) purification to

article:
Chunk # 3.
This is in consistence with the results of total protein determination using micro-
Lowry method However, the inner-membrane 
Figure 1 Sequential HepG2  cell culture captures at day-1 (A), day-3 (B) and day-6 (C)  110  Comparison of Three methods for mitochon dria isolation from human liver cell line 
 
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113  
 potential assay and mitochondrial activity assays 
indicated that the proportion of intact and active mitochondria in kit-based methods is more than manual method   
 
 
Figure 4 Comparison of membrane integrity of 
isolated mitochondria with three methods 
 
To the best of our knowledge, there are limited 
studies regarding the comparison of different methods for mitochondria l isolation Hartwig et al 
in 2011, published a technical brief on classical and kit based methods for isolation of mitochondria from mouse liver and compared the 
total mitochondria yield, mitochondrial activity and purity (4) Accordingly, we did not find any articles that compared such methods for isolation of mitochondria from human liver tissue or cell lines  
 
 
Figure 5 Comparison of ROS production of isolated 
mitochondria with three methods 
 
Since isolated mitochondria are targeted to 
transfer into new cells, several methods have been developed to transfer isolated mitochondria into 
 
Figure 3  mtDNA copy number amplification plot Curves (1) represent mtDNA amplification of samples isolated 
with Manual method Curves 1 and 3 represent mtDNA amplification of samples from MITOISO2 and Qproteome kits, respectively 
  Azimzadeh P et al  111 
Gastroenterol Hepatol Bed Bench 2016;9(2):105-113  live cells Microinjection is the most prevalent 
method and biochemical methods such as peptide mediated mitochondrial delivery (PMD) and liposome mediated transfer are now available (33) 
Sims et al in 1990, developed a rapid isolation 
method of active mitochondria from rat brain and it’s sub-regions (5) This study introduced two protocols based on discontinuous percoll gradient, 
for mitochondrial isolation according to further 
applications  
Pooreydy et al  offered a cost-effective and 
time-efficient method to isolate relatively pure mitochondria from PBMCs with focus on proteomics issues (34) The above-mentioned method seems to be cost effective and didn’t require special instruments such as ultracentrifuge Due to difference in quality assays between this study and our investigation, we haven’t compared it with results of the present study Several other studies have isolated the mitochondria from tissues and cells using manual methods consisted of sequential steps of homogenization, washing and centrifugation in 600g to 11000g (21, 22, 35)  
Added to these data, several studies have been 
used kit-based method for isolation of mitochondria especially for further injection strategies Elliot et al isolated mitochondria from 
MCF-12A human mammary gland cell line using MITOISO2 kit and transplanted the isolated mitochondria into live cells Their results showed that introduced mitochondria are acceptably active (19) Qproteome kit was also applied previously for isolation of mitochondria for both organelle transplantation and proteomics analysis (36)   
Main differences among three studied methods 
for mitochondrial isolation were in time efficiency, cost, equipment needed, quality and quantity of isolated mitochondria All three methods yielded a proportion of intact and active mitochondria With regard to these criteria, one can choose what method best suits their work 
Isolated mitochondria could be considered as 
an effective tool for proteomics analysis, mtDNA scrutiny and cell based therapies If a large amount 
of mitochondrial fractions for proteomics or such reasons is required, the manual method is the most suitable and cost effective method but if the purpose of isolation is to introduce the intact and active mitochondria into live cells using microinjection, biological or chemical methods, the Qproteome is the true choice Although, 
mitochondria isolated by MITOISO2 have been 
shown quite similar inner- membrane integrity but 
is provides mitochondria with significantly lower activity according to amount of ROS production 
References  
1 Becker LE Organelle pathology in metabolic 
neuromuscular disease: an overview Can J Vet Res 
1990; 54: 1-14 
2 Perier C, Vila M Mitochondrial biology and 
parkinson's disease Cold Spring Harb Perspect Med 
2012; 2: a009332 
3 Graham JM Purification of a crude mitochondrial 
fraction by density-gradient centrifugation Curr Protoc 
Cell Biol 2001; Chapter 3: Unit 3.4 
4 Hartwig S, Feckler C, Lehr S, Wallbrecht K, Wolgast 
H, Muller-Wieland D, et al A critical comparison 
between two classical and a kit-based method for 
mitochondria isolation Proteomics 2009; 9: 3209-14 
5 Sims NR Rapid isolation of metabolically active 
mitochondria from rat brain and subregions using 
Percoll density gradient centrifugation J Neurochem 1990; 55: 698-707 
6 Gatto L, Vizcaino JA, Hermjakob H, Huber W, 
Lilley KS Organelle proteomics experimental designs and analysis Proteomics 2010; 10: 3957-69 
7 Wiederhold E, Veenhoff LM, Poolman B, Slotboom 
DJ Proteomics of Saccharomyces cerevisiae 
Organelles Mol Cell Proteomics 2010; 9: 431-45

antiapoptotic properties of mitochondria from cultured

mitochondria.protocol:
**Steps for Mitochondrial Isolation:**

1. "To the best of our knowledge, there are limited studies regarding the comparison of different methods for mitochondrial isolation... Accordingly, we did not find any articles that compared such methods for isolation of mitochondria from human liver tissue or cell lines."

2. "Several other studies have isolated the mitochondria from tissues and cells using manual methods consisted of sequential steps of homogenization, washing and centrifugation in 600g to 11000g."

3. "Added to these data, several studies have been used kit-based method for isolation of mitochondria especially for further injection strategies."

4. "Main differences among three studied methods for mitochondrial isolation were in time efficiency, cost, equipment needed, quality and quantity of isolated mitochondria."

**Conclusion:**

The article mentions that there are limited studies comparing different methods for mitochondrial isolation from human liver tissue or cell lines. However, it does mention that several studies have used manual methods consisting of sequential steps of homogenization, washing, and centrifugation. Additionally, kit-based methods have been used for mitochondrial isolation, especially for injection strategies. The article highlights that the main differences among the studied methods are in time efficiency, cost, equipment needed, and the quality and quantity of isolated mitochondria. However, the exact steps for each method are not provided, and there may be additional protocol gaps that are not mentioned.

summary:
Summary (Iteration 7):
This study compares three methods, including the Lowry method, for isolating mitochondria from human liver cells. The HepG2 cell culture is used for mitochondrial activity assays and isolation. Kit-based methods, such as the MITOISO2 kit and Qproteome kit, show higher proportions of intact and active mitochondria compared to manual methods. The study also compares different methods for mitochondrial isolation from mouse liver. Various methods, such as peptide mediated mitochondrial delivery (PMD) and liposome mediated transfer, are used to transfer isolated mitochondria into new cells. The study introduces a rapid isolation method for active mitochondria from rat brain and its sub-regions using discontinuous percoll gradient centrifugation. A cost-effective and time-efficient method is established to isolate relatively pure mitochondria from PBMCs. Isolated mitochondria are useful for proteomics analysis, mtDNA scrutiny, and cell-based therapies. The study highlights the importance of total protein determination using the micro-Lowry method in assessing inner-membrane integrity and mitochondrial activity. Researchers such as Becker LE, Perier C, Graham JM, Hartwig S, Sims NR, Gatto L, Wiederhold E, Fox TD, and Panov A have made significant contributions to the field of mitochondrial research.

topics.topic: Methods for isolating mitochondria from human liver cells
topics.justification: The study compares different methods, including the Lowry method, the MITOISO2 kit, and the Qproteome kit, for isolating mitochondria from human liver cells.

topics.topic: Comparison of kit-based methods and manual methods for mitochondrial isolation
topics.justification: The study shows that kit-based methods, such as the MITOISO2 kit and the Qproteome kit, result in higher proportions of intact and active mitochondria compared to manual methods.

topics.topic: Methods for mitochondrial isolation from mouse liver
topics.justification: The study compares different methods, such as peptide mediated mitochondrial delivery (PMD) and liposome mediated transfer, for transferring isolated mitochondria into new cells.

topics.topic: Rapid isolation method for active mitochondria from rat brain and its sub-regions
topics.justification: The study introduces a rapid isolation method for active mitochondria from rat brain and its sub-regions using discontinuous percoll gradient centrifugation.

topics.topic: Cost-effective and time-efficient method for isolating mitochondria from PBMCs
topics.justification: The study establishes a cost-effective and time-efficient method for isolating relatively pure mitochondria from PBMCs.

topics.topic: Applications of isolated mitochondria
topics.justification: The study mentions that isolated mitochondria are useful for proteomics analysis, mtDNA scrutiny, and cell-based therapies.

topics.topic: Importance of total protein determination in assessing inner-membrane integrity and mitochondrial activity
topics.justification: The study highlights the importance of total protein determination using the micro-Lowry method in assessing inner-membrane integrity and mitochondrial activity.

topics.topic: Significant contributions to the field of mitochondrial research
topics.justification: The study mentions researchers such as Becker LE, Perier C, Graham JM, Hartwig S, Sims NR, Gatto L, Wiederhold E, Fox TD, and Panov A who have made significant contributions to the field of mitochondrial research.
